title: Differentiation
description: Learn the fundamental rules of finding derivatives, including the power rule, sum rule, and constant multiple rule.

contentBlocks.text: The derivative of a function $f(x)$ at a point $x$ measures the instantaneous rate of change of the function with respect to $x$. Geometrically, it represents the slope of the tangent line to the curve at that point.

One of the most useful rules in differentiation is the **Power Rule**: 

$$ rac{d}{dx} x^n = nx^{n-1}$$

This applies to any real number $n$.
